skill.name: agent-email-cli
skill.description: Operate the agent-email CLI to create disposable inboxes, poll for mail, retrieve message details, and manage local mailbox profiles for agent workflows.
skill.summary: Run agent-email mailbox workflows from Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
skill.author: zaddy6

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution hits and several high-severity weak-cryptography indicators. The weak-cryptography, filesystem, and reconnaissance hits are false positives from prose and shell examples, but the external command behavior is real and central to the skill. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found, so the skill is publishable with a warning about running a community CLI and handling mailbox content.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution and Global Package Installation
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE risk pattern. The skill instructs agents to check, install, and run agent-email and package-manager commands. This is expected for the skill, but it executes external binaries and may install a community npm package globally.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain explicit shell commands for installing and running agent-email. The behavior is legitimate for this CLI skill, but external command execution is confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Mailbox Content Access and Destructive Message Operations
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE contextual risk. The documented commands can read inbox metadata, retrieve full message bodies, and delete messages. This can expose verification links or codes if users point the tool at sensitive mailboxes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands directly cover read, show, and delete operations. The risk depends on user-selected inboxes, so it is moderate rather than high.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The flagged lines are skill descriptions or a reference to the show command, not cryptographic algorithm use. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic implementation in the scanned files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found prose and command names at the cited lines, not crypto code. The scanned files contain documentation only.

low_findings.title: Hidden Home Directory Static Hit Is a Benign PATH Note
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The cited line mentions ~/.bun/bin as a troubleshooting hint for command discovery. It does not read, write, enumerate, or exfiltrate hidden files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a single troubleshooting sentence about PATH configuration. No hidden file operation appears in the cited content.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The cited lines advise avoiding secrets, using machine-readable output, and listing mailbox accounts after an account error. They do not perform host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are operational guidance for the email CLI. No commands such as uname, whoami, env dumping, process listing, or network discovery were found there.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands Delegated to agent-email
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on shell commands that create, read, show, delete, list, and switch mailbox profiles. This is intentional, but agents should run it only when the user expects terminal email access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The static command-execution pattern is confirmed across the documented command examples. The commands are bounded to the agent-email workflow.

content.user_title: Manage Disposable Email from Agent Workflows
content.value_statement: Agent workflows often need temporary inbox access for verification messages and automated tests. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured way to use the agent-email CLI safely.
actual_capabilities: Check whether the agent-email CLI is available in the terminal.
Create a disposable mailbox account and capture non-secret identifiers.
Read the active inbox or a specified mailbox with optional waiting.
Retrieve a single message with full content when a message ID is known.
List, switch, and remove local mailbox profiles.
Delete a processed message when the user requests cleanup.
limitations: Requires the external agent-email CLI to be installed and available on PATH.
Can expose email contents if used with sensitive or personal inboxes.
Does not verify the trust or integrity of the npm package before installation.
Depends on the CLI service availability and its rate limits.

use_cases.title: Verify Test Accounts
use_cases.description: Create a disposable inbox and retrieve sign-up or password reset messages during automated testing.

use_cases.title: Support Agent Automation
use_cases.description: Let an agent wait for incoming mail, extract message IDs, and summarize non-secret fields.

use_cases.title: Manage Temporary Mailboxes
use_cases.description: List local mailbox profiles, switch the active inbox, and remove accounts that are no longer needed.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Mailbox
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agent-email to create a disposable mailbox. Return the email address and account ID, but do not include passwords or tokens.

prompt_templates.title: Read Recent Messages
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the default agent-email inbox and summarize the latest messages with sender, subject, created time, and message ID.

prompt_templates.title: Wait for Verification Mail
prompt_templates.prompt: Wait up to 30 seconds for a new verification email in the default inbox. Show the message ID and relevant non-secret summary.

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve and Clean Up
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the newest message in the selected mailbox, retrieve the full content, extract the requested verification detail, then delete the message if I confirm.

output_examples.input: Create a disposable mailbox for a test sign-up flow.
output_examples.output: Created a new mailbox and returned the email address, account ID, and active mailbox status. Secret values were omitted.

output_examples.input: Check the default inbox for the latest verification email.
output_examples.output: Reported the newest sender, subject, timestamp, and message ID. The summary avoided passwords, tokens, and raw message source.

output_examples.input: List my local agent-email accounts and switch to one address.
output_examples.output: Listed available mailbox profiles and switched the active profile after the requested address was identified.

best_practices: Use disposable mailboxes for automation instead of personal or production inboxes.
Summarize only the fields needed for the task and omit passwords or tokens.
Confirm before deleting messages or removing mailbox accounts.
anti_patterns: Do not paste full mailbox secrets, passwords, or tokens into chat responses.
Do not install or run the CLI in a production environment without review.
Do not use this skill to access mailboxes without clear user permission.

faq.question: Does this skill send email?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow creates mailboxes, reads incoming messages, shows message details, and manages local profiles.

faq.question: Does it require a separate CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. The agent-email command must be installed and available in the terminal environment.

faq.question: Can it wait for a new message?
faq.answer: Yes. The read command supports waiting and polling options for expected incoming mail.

faq.question: Can it read full message bodies?
faq.answer: Yes. The show command and full read option can retrieve detailed message content when needed.

faq.question: How are secrets handled?
faq.answer: The skill instructs agents not to print or summarize password and token fields from command output.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill runs external CLI commands and handles email content. That is intended, but it needs user awareness and environment care.
